data.body:
[G3 child-coverage — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S1 6d56: Faithful, lock-free snapshot materialization core)]

Audited this child's contribution to the parent's acceptance/success criteria; every absence cross-checked against the WHOLE EIGHT-child roster (S1 6d56 THIS, S2 70c7 cache, S2b b73c janitor, S3 fa49 gate threading, S4 30b3 attested signing, S4b e308 claim-gate ref-coherence, S5 b7fa CLI/MCP surfacing, S6 1ba5 docs). This child is the materialization PRIMITIVE — the riskiest piece, built first. The union of children covers every parent criterion. No uncovered parent criterion. EMPTY G3 findings list.

4-bucket coverage map (parent AC -> covering child/children; THIS child's role called out):
- Parent AC1 (no code-reading gate reads the server's working tree in attested mode; all read a faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly: THIS child is the PRIMARY OWNER of the FAITHFUL-MATERIALIZATION half — its AC1 (materialize(ref) produces a directory byte-matching the committed tree at the resolved SHA INCLUDING export-ignore-marked files, faithful not git archive), its AC2 (LFS pointer files detected not silently served; submodule handling defined+documented), its AC3 (atomic population — a reader never observes a partial tree; crashed materialization leaves only a tmp/ dir cleaned on startup). S3 (fa49 AC1/AC3/AC4) owns the complementary GATE RE-ROOTING half (point the gate's file tools at the snapshot dir, never the server working tree). No double-implementation; clean producer/consumer split. Heart of this child.
- Parent AC2 (a signed verdict binds verified_at_sha via the signed MANIFEST CHANNEL — no payload-schema/version change, prior signatures still verify — reproducible against that SHA) — FULLY covered by S4 (30b3 AC1/AC2 manifest-step binding + reproducible re-run). THIS child supplies the IMMUTABLE-SHA BASIS that makes reproducibility possible (its What: "resolve ref to an immutable SHA against origin"; its AC1 byte-faithful tree at that SHA), but does not sign (its Scope: "Out: ... signing (S4)"). STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence (S4 covers signing).
- Parent AC3 (local source mode reads the in-place checkout AND cannot produce a signed verdict; source=local is the documented back-out) — covered by S3 (fa49 AC4 reads in-place) + S4 (30b3 AC3 bars signing) + S6 (docs the back-out). THIS child contributes only the materialize-side mode behavior (its What: "attested mode fails closed, local mode still runs"; its Scope acknowledges source_mode param) but does not own the in-place read path or the signing bar. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence.
- Parent AC4 (concurrent gates on same/different SHAs run without git-lock contention or cross-op corruption; cache + janitor reclaim disk under a defined free-space watermark — S2 — without disrupting in-flight reads) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly: THIS child OWNS the GIT-LOCK-CONTENTION-FREE half — its AC6 (concurrent materialize() of DIFFERENT SHAs do not contend on index.lock/config.lock; via GIT_INDEX_FILE temp index + coalesced fetch as the only lock-taking step) + its AC3 (atomic population so no cross-op corruption / partial-tree read). S2 (70c7) owns single-flight + delete-on-last-close; S2b (b73c) owns the janitor reclamation under the free-space watermark. No gap, no double-ownership.
- Parent AC5 (private-repo fetch failures surface a descriptive, actionable credential error) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly: THIS child is the PRIMARY OWNER of the RAISING half — its AC5 (a private-repo fetch with missing credentials raises a descriptive, actionable error; attested mode fails closed) + its What ("a fetch that fails on auth surfaces an actionable credential message (see S6), not a raw git error"). S6 (1ba5 AC1/AC2) owns the DOCUMENTATION half (state the requirement + how to supply + message+remedy). Clean raiser/documenter split; no uncovered half.
- Parent AC6 (portable across macOS + Linux: no external tar; no hardcoded /tmp; flock+mkdir fallback) — FULLY covered, SPLIT cleanly: THIS child is the PRIMARY OWNER of the IMPLEMENTATION half — its AC4 (no external tar binary invoked; no hardcoded /tmp; runs on macOS + Linux) + its What (extraction never shells to external tar; temp root from tempfile.gettempdir()/mkdtemp 0700, overridable via REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR, OUTSIDE the repo/.git; fcntl.flock with atomic-mkdir fallback on a local FS). S6 (1ba5 AC5) owns the DOCS half (REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR + EFS/NFS flock caveat). Clean impl/docs split. Heart of this child's portability seam.
- Parent AC7 (operational tunables — free-space watermark, default ref, grace/age windows — configurable with documented defaults, S2/S3) — FULLY covered by S2b (b73c AC9 watermark/grace/max-age/reverify configurable with documented defaults) + S3 (fa49 ref/source defaults via standard rebar config + REBAR_* precedence) + S6 (docs). THIS child establishes the default ref concept (default origin/main) and the REBAR_GATE_TMPDIR override, but the operational-tunables-with-documented-defaults obligation is owned by S2b/S3. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence (S2b/S3 cover it).
- Parent AC8 (an ADR captures the architecture + rejected alternatives — PID-lease, payload-schema SHA binding) — FULLY covered by S2/S2b (b73c AC10 ADR records cache/janitor architecture + rejected PID+heartbeat lease) + S4 (30b3 AC1 manifest-vs-payload SHA-binding decision in the ADR) + S4b (e308 ADR 0002). The parent ADR note also lists "read-tree+checkout-index over git archive" and "delete-on-last-close" as captured patterns — the read-tree/checkout-index pattern ORIGINATES in THIS child's What, but the parent explicitly assigns ADR AUTHORING to S2/S4 ("authored in S2/S4"), not this primitive child. THIS child's own AC set carries no ADR-authoring obligation; the design it embodies is captured by the named ADR-authoring siblings. STRUCTURAL w.r.t. this child. Not an absence (S2/S2b/S4 author the ADR covering this child's patterns).
- Parent AC9 (all EIGHT enumerated child stories closed; make lint + full test suite green) — STRUCTURAL/meta epic-completion criterion; covered by the existence of all eight children in the roster (this child is S1, the de-risking-first primitive) + each child's own green-bar obligation. Not an absence at this child.

Success-criteria narrative ("Every code-reading gate verifies a client-specified, immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout — safely under concurrent distributed use; signed verdicts bind verified_at_sha via the manifest channel (reproducible, no prior-signature breakage); local mode preserves the single-developer verify-before-push workflow") decomposes onto AC1-AC6. The "immutable, reproducible snapshot at a pinned SHA — never the server's mutable checkout" clause rests on THIS child's core deliverable: resolve-ref->immutable-SHA + FAITHFUL byte-for-byte materialization of the committed tree (read-tree + checkout-index, not the lossy git archive) into an atomic, content-addressed snapshot, with no index.lock/config.lock contention so it holds "safely under concurrent distributed use." The "reproducible / verified_at_sha / signed" clause is carried by S4 atop this immutable-SHA basis; the "client-specified" surfacing is S5; the "never the server's mutable checkout" gate-side re-rooting is S3; "local preserves verify-before-push" is S3/S4/S6. No uncovered obligation introduced by this child's existence.

Named-consumer / delegation cross-checks (covered-by-named-consumer, not absences):
- This child's Scope explicitly delegates the cache/janitor to S2 ("Out: the cache/janitor (S2)"), gate wiring to S3, signing to S4, surfaces to S5 — each claimed by a present named sibling. The credential-error MESSAGE detail is cross-referenced to S6 ("see S6"; S6 AC1/AC2 claim it). The submodule/LFS handling this child DEFINES is documented by this child itself (its AC2) and surfaced operationally by S6 docs — no unclaimed downstream.
- The fetch-prerequisites this child documents (uploadpack.allowReachableSHA1InWant; --filter=blob:none) are self-contained operational notes on this child's own fetch step, not a hand-off requiring a sibling claim.

Note (scope, NOT a G3 finding): the standing G4 dependency-gap on sibling S4 (30b3) — routing the pinned-SHA snapshot handle (this child's materialize() output) into plan_review/attest.py's dependency_hashes()/_rehash()/claim-gate freshness re-check across the S3<->S4/S4b seam — is a CONSISTENCY (G4) concern about WHO threads this child's snapshot handle into the attestation hashing path, NOT a G3 union-coverage gap. The corresponding parent obligations (AC1 snapshot-only reads, AC2 reproducibility, the ref-coherence clause delegated to S4b) remain CLAIMED by children, so the union covers them; the WHO-threads-it question lives in the G4 channel and is unrelated to this primitive child's own coverage.

Conclusion: this child cleanly covers the materialization-primitive slice of the parent criteria — it is the PRIMARY OWNER of the faithful-materialization half of parent AC1 (byte-faithful read-tree+checkout-index incl. export-ignore, LFS detection, submodule handling, atomic population), the git-lock-contention-free + no-cross-op-corruption half of AC4, the credential-error-RAISING half of AC5, and the portability IMPLEMENTATION half of AC6; it supplies the immutable-SHA basis underpinning AC2's reproducibility. No parent acceptance/success criterion is left uncovered by the roster. No G3 finding for this pairing.
